pi_items.source: Verified against setup_task.sh and export_result.sh - consistent file path used throughout task scripts
Verified against setup_task.sh and export_result.sh - consistent with docker-jitsi-meet deployment structure
Verified against task.json description and export_result.sh

Jitsi Meet official documentation and GitHub - config.stereo = true is a real configuration option for enabling stereo audio. Reference: https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/

Jitsi Meet official documentation and GitHub issues confirm config.p2p = { enabled: false } is the correct syntax to disable P2P mode. Reference: https://github.com/jitsi/jitsi-meet, https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/

Jitsi Meet documentation confirms Opus bitrate range is 6000-510000 bps. 510000 is the maximum allowed value. Reference: https://github.com/jitsi/jitsi-meet/issues/7384, https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/

Jitsi Meet official documentation confirms enableNoisyMicDetection is a real configuration option that can be set to false to disable noisy microphone detection. Reference: https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/, https://github.com/jitsi/jitsi-meet/issues/12986

privileged_info_summary: The task configures Jitsi Meet for high-fidelity music instruction using verified audio settings. All metadata claims are correct: stereo audio is enabled via config.stereo = true, P2P mode is disabled with config.p2p = { enabled: false }, the Opus codec is set to its maximum bitrate of 510000 bps (the maximum allowed value in Jitsi Meet), and noisy microphone detection is disabled via enableNoisyMicDetection = false. These are all real Jitsi Meet configuration options documented in the official Jitsi Meet configuration guide.
